talk_metadata.talk_title: Advances on Representation Learning and Digital Pathology
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we set out to control for detailed tumor subtype, harnessing a large dataset, to identify what role digital pathology has as a complement to, rather than just as a surrogate for, clinical sequencing"
Speaker noted: "Most molecular alterations don't associate with characteristic phenotypes, but about 4% do"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Digital pathology inference of molecular alterations
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited phenotype-genotype correlation for >96% of molecular features

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: AEON and Paladin deep learning models trained on H&E images
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed reliance on a forked version of the CLAM package and custom preprocessing, suggesting limited proprietary protection
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad label approach without biomarker stratification may dilute signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that most molecular alterations lack a phenotype on H&E (observation) yet positioned digital pathology as a complementary diagnostic tool (conclusion)

key_data_gaps: Limited external validation beyond TCGA and one curated cohort

crispro_opportunity.description: Only ~4% of molecular alterations show a detectable phenotype on H&E, indicating a need for more sensitive biomarker integration.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Most molecular alterations don't associate with characteristic phenotypes, but about 4% do"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi-omics signatures to predict phenotype-genotype links beyond visual pathology.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine AEON predictions with genomic classifiers (e.g., GDENS) to improve subtype accuracy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker compared AEON to GDENS and noted performance gains with multimodal integration.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO platform can fuse imaging and sequencing data to generate unified predictive models.

cited_competitors.name: GDENS
cited_competitors.context: Genomic classifier used as benchmark for subtype inference

unresolved_questions: Can allele‑specific mutations be distinguished by the H&E‑based models?
How will the models perform on prospective clinical cohorts with diverse treatment regimens?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Multi‑omic Model for Glioblastoma Recurrence Prediction
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the goal of my project is to incorporate information from all of these modalities, but focus my algorithm on the types of data that is routinely collectible"
Speaker admitted: "It's not something that we've looked at yet. It's definitely on the list" when asked about allele‑specific mutations

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Model relies on publicly available Uni2 architecture; no disclosed novel IP
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Small pilot cohort (19 patients) limits generalizability

key_data_gaps: Limited treatment heterogeneity (only 4 patients received PD‑1 inhibitor)

crispro_opportunity.description: Need to predict recurrence using only H&E and MRI when spatial omics are unavailable.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker emphasized democratizing use of spatial modalities by inferring gene/protein patterns from histopathology.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate predictive signatures from routine imaging linked to underlying molecular programs.

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO’s ability to integrate heterogeneous modalities (MRI, histology, limited spatial data) to improve recurrence prediction.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described multi‑modal integration framework with attention heads for recurrence prediction.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s modular architecture can ingest MRI radiomics and pathology embeddings jointly.

unresolved_questions: How will the model perform across diverse GBM treatment regimens?
Can the attention maps be validated against independent spatial transcriptomics?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Single‑cell and Spatial Atlas of Prostate Cancer Progression
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we provide a unified single‑cell and spatial framework to map cellular ecosystem across PCa progression"
Speaker noted: "SP1 macrophage are associated with poor clinical outcomes"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects of anti‑SP1 therapy not explored in normal tissues
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker described pre‑clinical efficacy but did not discuss safety profile

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: SP1 macrophage mediated immunosuppression in bone metastasis
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mouse model only; no patient stratification by SP1 expression
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline SP1 macrophage levels in patients
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small animal cohort, no power calculation reported

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: No disclosed novel IP; relies on standard CRISPR knockout strategies
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad targeting of SP1 may affect normal macrophage functions

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed SP1 macrophage enrichment correlates with poor outcome (observation) yet proposed systemic SP1 inhibition without addressing potential systemic immune effects (conclusion)

key_data_gaps: Lack of human clinical trial data for anti‑SP1 therapy
No validation of SP1 targeting in diverse patient cohorts

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop CrisPRO‑guided small‑molecule or peptide inhibitors that selectively disrupt SP1‑macrophage signaling while sparing normal macrophages.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted SP1 macrophage as a promising therapeutic target but noted pre‑clinical status only.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model SP1‑related regulatory networks to identify druggable nodes with minimal off‑target risk.

crispro_opportunity.description: Pair SP1 inhibition with TGF‑β blockade to overcome compensatory immunosuppressive pathways.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed TGF‑β signaling from AC‑like cells to macrophages.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic effects of combined pathway inhibition using integrated multi‑omics data.

unresolved_questions: What is the safety profile of systemic SP1 inhibition in humans?
Can SP1 macrophage levels be reliably measured in patient biopsies for stratification?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Deep Learning Profiling for Regulatory Somatic Variants Across Clonal Cancer Evolution
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "severity of the regulatory score is important for the survival and progression"
Speaker noted: "regulatory variant count itself is not significant"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Model does not account for epigenetic context or 3D genome architecture

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Built on publicly available deep‑learning models; limited proprietary claims
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Relies on bulk sequencing; may miss low‑frequency subclonal events

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated strong association between regulatory disruption severity and outcome (observation) yet suggested the approach can be applied pan‑cancer without accounting for tumor‑type specific regulatory landscapes (conclusion)

key_data_gaps: Limited functional validation of predicted regulatory effects

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to prioritize regulatory variants that drive resistance and design CRISPR‑based interventions.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that later clonal variants have higher regulatory scores and may drive progression.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate the impact of editing specific regulatory sites on downstream expression programs.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate regulatory disruption scores as stratification biomarkers in upcoming trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed severity score predicts progression‑free interval across TCGA and ICGC cohorts.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate patient‑specific disruption profiles for enrollment criteria.

cited_competitors.name: NetMHC 2.0 Pan
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a benchmark for MHC‑II binding, not directly related but cited in discussion

unresolved_questions: How do regulatory disruption scores perform in prospective cohorts with treatment heterogeneity?
Can the model be extended to incorporate chromatin accessibility data?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial Multi‑omics for Glioblastoma Cell‑Based Therapy and Intra‑operative Decision Making
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "mass spectrometry can distinguish IDH‑wildtype glioblastoma intra‑operatively"
Speaker admitted: "we are still in exploratory analysis and need validation for MS‑Pen signatures"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects on systemic macrophage function not evaluated
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted only pre‑clinical mouse data showing survival benefit
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: No safety data presented for engineered macrophages

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Upcoming engineered macrophage trial (not yet NCT listed)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Resistance of glioblastoma to therapy via TGF‑β mediated immunosuppression
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker‑driven selection for tumors with high mesenchymal or AC‑like signatures
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline MS‑Pen metabolic signature or SP1 macrophage level
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early‑phase trial without power calculation disclosed

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Technology is collaborative with Baylor; limited exclusive IP disclosed
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Requires specialized intra‑operative equipment and trained personnel
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Relies on metabolic signatures that may vary across patients and institutions

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that mesenchymal‑like cells are enriched in hypoxic cores (observation) yet suggested intra‑operative metabolic detection can guide resection without confirming that metabolites uniquely map to those cell states (conclusion)

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective validation of MS‑Pen signatures in patients
No clinical safety data for engineered macrophages

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to integrate spatial transcriptomics, metabolomics, and proteomics for robust intra‑operative decision support.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described multi‑omics integration to identify metabolic signatures of plasticity‑associated states.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse heterogeneous spatial layers into a unified predictive model for resection guidance.

crispro_opportunity.description: Pair engineered macrophages with metabolic inhibitors identified by CrisPRO to enhance anti‑tumor immunity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted TGF‑β mediated immunosuppression and metabolic niches.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict synergistic metabolic‑immune interventions.

unresolved_questions: How reproducible are the MS‑Pen metabolic signatures across surgical centers?
What is the optimal threshold for metabolic signature‑guided resection to balance safety and efficacy?

talk_metadata.talk_title: ADT Norm: Non‑parametric Normalization for Single‑Cell Proteomics Across Technologies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We propose this model called ADT Norm... to align all those background signal, all those valley, and all those positive signal"
Speaker admitted: "there are biological variation that get rid of because... if your goal is cell type annotation you may lose activation signals"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential loss of biologically relevant activation states during aggressive background alignment

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Method described in a Nature Communications paper; no clear patent claim
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Normalization may obscure subtle disease‑related protein expression changes

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ADT Norm improves batch mixing (observation) yet cautioned that it may remove biologically meaningful activation differences (conclusion)

key_data_gaps: Limited demonstration on clinical outcome prediction
No assessment of impact on downstream therapeutic biomarker discovery

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate ADT Norm into multi‑center proteomics trials to ensure consistent cell‑type annotation across sites.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted batch effects across datasets and the need for non‑parametric alignment.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can incorporate ADT Norm as a preprocessing step within its multi‑omics pipeline.

crispro_opportunity.description: Recover lost activation signals by applying CrisPRO’s variance‑preserving de‑convolution after ADT Norm.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted loss of activation information post‑normalization.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model residual biological variance to re‑extract activation signatures.

cited_competitors.name: SeR normalization
cited_competitors.context: Baseline method for ADT count scaling

unresolved_questions: How does ADT Norm perform on rare cell populations with low marker expression?
Can the method be extended to integrate CyTOF and imaging mass cytometry data without loss of spatial context?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Foundation‑Model‑Based Prediction of MHC‑II Peptide Binding
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we achieve state‑of‑the‑art performance using only ~2% of the training data"
Speaker admitted: "AlphaFold sometimes hallucinates peptide orientation for HLA‑DP"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: AlphaFold3 structural predictions for HLA‑DP peptide orientation
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Incorrect peptide orientation leading to reduced binding prediction accuracy
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted unexpected orientation reduces AUC for HLA‑DP alleles

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Proprietary assay but limited to DRB1 alleles in current dataset
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Requires specialized peptide library synthesis and sequencing
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Training data biased toward DRB1, limiting generalizability to rare HLA‑II alleles

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated high AUC for DRB1 alleles (observation) yet suggested the model is ready for rare HLA‑II prediction without additional data (conclusion)

key_data_gaps: Limited PepSeq data for HLA‑DP and HLA‑DQ alleles
No prospective validation in patient cohorts

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to re‑train orientation‑aware models for HLA‑DP peptides, correcting AlphaFold hallucinations.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted orientation errors reducing predictive performance for HLA‑DP.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can incorporate orientation constraints into graph neural networks.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine PepSeq‑derived binding data with CrisPRO’s expression‑aware modeling to predict immunogenicity.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed need for antigen availability and expression data.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO integrates gene expression with binding predictions for holistic immune response modeling.

cited_competitors.name: NetMHC‑2.0 Pan
cited_competitors.context: Benchmark model compared against the new GNN approach

cited_competitors.name: Maria
cited_competitors.context: State‑of‑the‑art deep learning model that underperformed in benchmarks

unresolved_questions: How will the model perform on HLA‑II alleles with no PepSeq training data?
Can orientation correction be generalized across all HLA‑II loci?
